description_long: This is a hand-crafted, round ceramic bowl with a rough, textured exterior and a slightly uneven, wavy rim. The bowl's surface is predominantly a light beige or tan color, with some darker, smoky discoloration near the rim and interior, suggesting it was fired in a traditional kiln. The interior is darkened, possibly from use or intentional design, and the rim features several small chips and irregularities. On one side, there is a visible inscription in white, indicating it was made by a Cherokee artisan in Swain County, North Carolina, in 1886. The bowl appears to be of moderate size, suitable for holding food or other items, and has a rustic, utilitarian appearance. There are no visible handles or additional features beyond the inscribed text and the natural, hand-formed shape.
description: A rustic, hand-crafted ceramic bowl with a rough beige exterior, darkened interior, and an inscribed text on one side. The rim is uneven and slightly chipped, giving it a traditional, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: This view shows the inscribed text on the bowl's side, the uneven rim, and the darkened interior.
description_view_1: The bowl's rough, textured exterior and wavy rim are visible, with no inscription present on this side.
description_view_2: This angle highlights the bowl's rounded shape, rough surface, and the darkened interior, with no visible text.
description_view_3: The bowl's rim appears most uneven here, and a small label or mark is visible on the inner rim, with the exterior texture clearly shown.
attribute_decorative_patterns: inscribed text on one side; no other decorative patterns
